Now, it does still amaze me how I'll just be going about my day and out of nowhere, a memory from my career will come to my mind. And on most occasions, when it does occur, it isn't a pleasant memory, unfortunately. In the video that we just watched reminds me of when I had to deal with people who were drunk and aggressive. And those things are already bad enough. Adding a weapon to the situation can take it to an entirely different level because many times there is no reasoning with these people. And I did have to deal with people who are intoxicated while carrying weapons. And I'm thankful that my partners and I could rely on each other because if we couldn't, a simple call could have turned into a nightmare scenario. Now, today, guys, as soon as the video starts, we see police at a hotel and they have the task of dealing with some people who appear intoxicated. And thank goodness no one grabbed the weapon and that it didn't go off when it fell. Because contrary to what people might think, that can happen when a weapon falls. And it's called inertia discharge, and it can happen with semi-automatic pistols and with some revolvers. And then after a struggle, the officer had to arrest this woman, and that's because she allegedly had a gun in a bar. And notice how this female officer had to explain to this woman numerous times why she was in custody, which in my opinion, shows how irresponsible this woman was to have a gun in a bar because she does appear drunk. Now, lastly, in the end, both suspects were arrested. And if you get nothing else from this, remember, carrying a weapon is an awesome responsibility, and it shouldn't be taken lightly.
